lint.output: ruff found 8 issues: 2x F401 (unused imports), 1x F811 (redefined name), 5x I001 (unsorted imports)

findings.title: Missing email allowlist validation for gallery access
findings.description: The gallery authentication endpoint (/auth/view) does not validate client emails against a per-gallery allowlist or domain-based access control. Currently, any syntactically valid email receives a JWT token for any gallery, which effectively grants unauthenticated access to galleries. This is a significant authorization bypass vulnerability.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement email allowlist validation: (1) Add 'allowed_emails' and/or 'allowed_domains' fields to the gallery DynamoDB item, (2) In handle_view_auth(), after validating the gallery exists, check if the provided email is in the allowlist before issuing a JWT, (3) Return 403 Forbidden if email is not authorized.

findings.title: No JWT revocation mechanism for gallery admin tokens
findings.description: Gallery admin JWTs are issued with long TTLs and have no revocation mechanism. If an admin account is compromised or an admin is removed, their token remains valid until expiration. The code acknowledges this limitation (lines 1024-1027 in admin/handler.py) and has reduced the TTL from 30 to 7 days as a temporary mitigation, but this is not a proper solution.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement token revocation: (1) Add a token revocation check in authenticate() function before accepting a JWT, (2) Option A: Implement a revocation table that tracks invalidated token JTI claims with expiration, or (3) Option B: Use short-lived access tokens (15 min) with a refresh token flow. The code already has CAT-022 comment about re-validating account status (line 406) - extend this to also check a revocation list.

findings.title: Duplicate uuid import in auth_view handler
findings.description: The uuid module is imported twice in lambda/auth_view/handler.py - once on line 9 and again on line 25. This creates a redefined name (F811 linter warning) and indicates disorganized imports.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove the duplicate import on line 25 and consolidate all imports at the top of the file in the standard order: stdlib imports first, then third-party imports, then local imports.

findings.title: Demo key validation uses unbounded table scan without pagination limit
findings.description: The _validate_demo_key() function in admin/handler.py uses _scan_all() to retrieve demo sessions, which scans the entire table. The code includes a comment explaining why Limit=1 cannot be used (lines 361-362), but the current implementation could be inefficient if the demo_sessions table grows large.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a Global Secondary Index (GSI) on the demo_sessions table with demo_key as the partition key. Then use query() instead of scan(). This would reduce the operation from O(n) to O(1) or O(log n). Alternatively, use FilterExpression with a Limit on the number of pages returned (e.g., limit to 10 items scanned before returning).

findings.title: Very short gallery listings cache TTL (2 seconds) may impact performance
findings.description: The public gallery listings cache in auth_view/handler.py uses a 2-second TTL (line 519) to prevent cross-tenant leaks in multi-origin setups. While the conservative TTL is understandable for security, it may cause excessive DynamoDB scans under high concurrency.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider increasing the cache TTL to 5-10 seconds and evaluating whether cross-tenant leaks are actually a risk in the deployment environment. If multi-origin setup is indeed a risk, consider caching per-origin or per-request-context instead of globally. Monitor DynamoDB scan metrics to validate the impact.

findings.title: Email validation regex patterns are inconsistent across codebase
findings.description: Email validation is performed using different regex patterns in different files, which could lead to inconsistent validation logic and potential security issues.
findings.fix_suggestion: Define a single canonical email validation regex at module level (or in a shared constants file) and use it consistently across all handlers. The admin/handler.py pattern is more restrictive and preferred. Create a utility function email_is_valid(email) in each handler or import from a shared module.

findings.title: Bare exception handling in JWT verification could hide real errors
findings.description: Multiple places catch broad exceptions without specific error handling, particularly in JWT verification and secret loading. This can hide real errors and make debugging difficult.
findings.fix_suggestion: Catch specific exceptions: (1) ValueError for JSON decode errors, (2) KeyError for missing JWT fields, (3) TypeError for type mismatches. Add logging for unexpected exceptions before returning None so errors can be diagnosed in production.

findings.title: Unused imports in test files (F401)
findings.description: Two test files have unused imports that should be removed: tests/unit/test_zip_privacy.py imports BytesIO and MagicMock but doesn't use them.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove the unused imports: remove BytesIO from line 5, and remove MagicMock from line 6 (keep patch).

findings.title: Unsorted import blocks in test files (I001)
findings.description: Five test files have unsorted or unformatted import blocks, triggering ruff I001 warnings. These should be organized according to isort/ruff conventions.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run 'ruff format tests/' to auto-fix import ordering in all test files. Or manually reorganize imports to group stdlib, then third-party, then local imports alphabetically within each group.

findings.title: Potential cache collision risk in globals without clear expiration semantics
findings.description: Multiple handlers use module-level caches (e.g., _jwt_secret_cache, _galleries_response_cache) without clear semantics about what happens when cache expires. The cache expiration logic is duplicated across files.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider extracting cache management into a helper utility class with consistent expiration semantics, or use a simple cache decorator. Alternatively, document the cache invalidation strategy clearly in comments.

findings.title: Magic numbers scattered throughout handlers without clear semantics
findings.description: Various magic numbers are used throughout the handlers without clear explanation (e.g., bcrypt rounds=12, timeout values, cache TTLs, max attempts). These should be documented or centralized.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add comments explaining why specific values were chosen. Group related constants (e.g., all timeout values) and document in a constants section at the top of each handler.

lint.output: 40 linter errors found by ruff: 35 fixable with --fix. Errors include 28 unused imports (F401), 1 unused variable (F841), 3 ambiguous variable names (E741), and 8 other issues.

findings.title: 40 linter errors - unused imports and variables
findings.description: Ruff linter detected 40 errors across the codebase. Most are unused imports (F401) and unused variables (F841). 35 errors are automatically fixable with ruff --fix.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run 'python3 -m ruff check . --fix' to automatically remove unused imports and variables. Manually rename ambiguous variable names like 'l' to clearer names like 'lane'.

findings.title: Broad exception handling masks real errors
findings.file: backoffice/regression.py, backoffice/server.py, backoffice/sync/engine.py, backoffice/sync/providers/aws.py, backoffice/setup.py, tests/test_servers.py
findings.description: 6 files use broad 'except Exception:' or bare 'except:' clauses that catch all exceptions including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt, making debugging difficult.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Replace broad except clauses with specific exception types:
- Instead of 'except Exception:', use 'except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError)'
- Use 'except FileNotFoundError:' instead of bare except
- Add explicit re-raise for unexpected exceptions

findings.title: Use of shell=True in subprocess.run()
findings.description: backoffice/regression.py line 96 uses subprocess.run() with shell=True. While commands come from config file, this is a security anti-pattern vulnerable to injection if config format changes.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Use list-based command passing instead of shell=True. Replace:
subprocess.run('python3 -m pytest --cov=.', shell=True)
With:
subprocess.run(['python3', '-m', 'pytest', '--cov', '.'], shell=False)
For dynamic commands, use shlex.split() to safely tokenize.

findings.title: Potential CORS logic issue in api_server.py
findings.description: The CORS header logic has a subtle issue: if '*' is in allowed_origins, the code echoes back the origin without validating it, defeating CORS protection.

findings.title: Missing error handling for subprocess calls
findings.description: Multiple subprocess.run() calls in api_server.py and server.py do not check return codes. Agent script failures are silently ignored.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Check return codes and log failures:
result = subprocess.run(args, cwd=str(r), capture_output=True)
if result.returncode != 0:
    logger.error(f"Agent {dept} failed: {result.stderr}")

findings.title: Race condition in running_jobs tracking
findings.description: The running_jobs dict is checked under lock, but the thread starts after lock is released, creating a window for duplicate job starts.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Start the thread inside the lock:
with running_lock:
    if dept in running_jobs:
        return False
    t = threading.Thread(...)
    running_jobs[dept] = t
    t.start()  # <-- Inside lock

findings.title: Ambiguous variable names in tests
findings.description: tests/test_delivery.py uses 'l' (lowercase letter L) as loop variable in list comprehensions. This is ambiguous with '1' and violates PEP 8.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Rename 'l' to 'lane':
sprint_now = next(lane for lane in result['sprint_lanes'] if lane['lane'] == 'Sprint Now')

findings.title: Unnecessary environment dict copy in subprocess
findings.description: backoffice/regression.py line 100 uses {**os.environ} creating an unnecessary dict copy. subprocess.run() uses os.environ by default.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Remove the env parameter or pass None:
Before: subprocess.run(..., env={**os.environ})
After: subprocess.run(...)
Or: subprocess.run(..., env=None)

findings.title: Some functions lack comprehensive docstrings
findings.description: Some public functions in aggregate.py and sync/engine.py have minimal docstrings without parameter or return documentation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add comprehensive docstrings with parameter and return type documentation following Google or NumPy format.

tests.output:
> bible-app@0.1.0 test
> vitest run

RUN  v4.0.18 /home/merm/projects/bible-app

Test Files  12 passed (12)
Tests  124 passed (124)
Duration  3.16s

findings.title: Local data is not scoped per signed-in user, so one user's private data can leak into another account
findings.description: The local persistence layer uses global keys like `selah_studies`, `selah_journals`, and `selah_bookmarks` for every browser session instead of namespacing by user ID. Signing out only clears the auth token and cached encryption material, so the next person who signs in on the same browser inherits the previous user's local data set. The settings page then backs up the entire global export into whichever account is currently signed in.
findings.fix_suggestion: Namespace all local storage keys by a stable user scope such as authenticated user ID or an anonymous device ID, and migrate existing data. At minimum, clear user-owned local data on logout/account switch and prevent account backup from exporting records created under a different user scope.

findings.title: Account backup silently uploads plaintext journals whenever the encryption key is unavailable
findings.description: The backup path only encrypts journal fields if `masterKey` is present. The settings page always allows account backup and passes `masterKey` as-is, including `null`. That means any signed-in session that has lost or not unlocked its in-memory key will upload raw journal content to the server even though the product claims journal entries are zero-knowledge encrypted and unreadable by the server.
findings.fix_suggestion: Fail closed: block account backup until encryption is ready for any journal export, prompt the user to unlock first, and never send plaintext journal content to the API from this backup path.

findings.title: Encrypted journals are written to DynamoDB but the read API drops the encrypted payload
findings.description: The journal write handler persists `_encrypted`, `_encVersion`, and `_payload` for encrypted entries, but the read handler only maps plaintext fields like `content`, `prompts`, and `tags`. For encrypted rows, those fields are absent, so the GET response omits the only recoverable journal body. This makes server-backed journal recovery and multi-device sync impossible for encrypted entries.
findings.fix_suggestion: Return `_encrypted`, `_encVersion`, `_payload`, and preserved metadata for encrypted entries, and add an integration test that saves an encrypted journal and then reads back the exact encrypted blob.

findings.title: Multiple DynamoDB list endpoints read only the first page and silently truncate larger accounts
findings.description: User-facing read handlers for studies, journals, bookmarks, daily verses, and stats issue a single `QueryCommand` and never follow `LastEvaluatedKey`. DynamoDB caps a query page at roughly 1 MB, so once an account grows beyond that size the API will start returning partial datasets and incorrect counts without any error. The account deletion path in the same file already demonstrates the correct pagination loop, which these handlers do not use.
findings.fix_suggestion: Extract a reusable paginated query helper that loops on `LastEvaluatedKey`, and use it for all collection reads and count computations. For stats, prefer `Select: 'COUNT'` with pagination or precomputed counters.

findings.title: Password reset and email verification codes are stored in plaintext
findings.description: The API writes six-digit reset and verification codes directly into DynamoDB and later compares them as plaintext. Anyone with read access to the table, backups, or debugging exports can use live codes to reset passwords or verify accounts during their validity window. These secrets should be treated like authentication tokens and stored only as hashes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Hash reset and verification codes before storage, compare hashes in constant time, and keep only metadata like expiry and attempt counts in plaintext.

lint.output: Astro type checking passed: ok (no errors)

findings.title: h3 dependency has multiple path traversal and injection vulnerabilities
findings.description: The h3 package (version <=1.15.8) has been identified with multiple high-severity vulnerabilities including path traversal via percent-encoded dot segments, SSE event injection, and double decoding bypass attacks. These vulnerabilities could allow arbitrary file reads and event stream injection attacks.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run 'npm audit fix' to update h3 to a patched version. Ensure all devDependencies are updated to their latest secure versions.

findings.title: devalue dependency has prototype pollution vulnerability
findings.description: The devalue package (version <=5.6.3) contains a prototype pollution vulnerability in devalue.parse and devalue.unflatten functions. This vulnerability allows emission of objects with __proto__ own properties, which could lead to unintended property pollution.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run 'npm audit fix' to update devalue to a patched version that addresses the prototype pollution vulnerability.

findings.title: lodash dependency has prototype pollution in _.unset and _.omit
findings.description: The lodash package (versions 4.0.0 - 4.17.21) contains a prototype pollution vulnerability in the _.unset and _.omit functions. This vulnerability is present as a transitive dependency through yaml-language-server → @astrojs/language-server.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run 'npm audit fix' to update lodash and its dependents to patched versions. May require updating @astrojs/check or other Astro tooling.

findings.title: Content Security Policy uses unsafe-inline for script-src
findings.description: The Base.astro layout includes a Content-Security-Policy meta tag that allows unsafe-inline for script-src. While this is necessary for the inline Plausible analytics script, it weakens the overall XSS protection provided by CSP.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider using a nonce-based CSP instead of unsafe-inline. Update the Plausible script initialization to use a nonce attribute. The current implementation is acceptable for a static site, but nonce-based CSP would be more secure.

findings.title: Email validation uses basic regex pattern
findings.description: The contact form email validation uses a simple regex pattern for client-side validation. While this pattern catches obvious invalid emails, it does not strictly conform to RFC 5322 email format specifications.
findings.fix_suggestion: The current regex is acceptable for client-side validation. Server-side validation on the AWS Lambda endpoint is what truly matters for security.

findings.title: Parallax effect animation may impact performance on low-end devices
findings.description: The index.astro implements a custom parallax effect using requestAnimationFrame with scroll listeners. While implemented with throttling, this could still impact performance on older devices.
findings.fix_suggestion: The current implementation with requestAnimationFrame and passive listeners is already optimized. This is acceptable as-is.

lint.output: No linter configured. Using pylint/flake8 checks during code review.

findings.title: Bare except clause catching all exceptions without logging
findings.description: Multiple exception handlers use bare `except Exception:` without logging errors, which silently hides failures and makes debugging difficult. This masks real errors and security issues.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add logging for all exception handlers: `except Exception as e: logger.error(f'License secret check failed: {e}')`

findings.title: Password stored in plaintext in environment before Secret Manager write
findings.description: Console password is passed through environment variable `console_password` in request body and temporarily stored in plaintext in Python memory/Cloud Build substitutions before being written to Secret Manager. The password string is also visible in audit logs and build metadata.
findings.fix_suggestion:
1) Remove password from metadata completely
2) Never log or include password in build substitutions
3) Store only in Secret Manager immediately
4) Use `bytes` type to prevent accidental string conversion in logs

findings.title: Multiple bare except clauses swallowing all exceptions
findings.description: Eight functions use bare `except Exception:` without any logging or error propagation. This includes critical paths like passphrase loading (_get_passphrase line 563), Google token verification (line 578), and quota capacity checks (line 649).
findings.fix_suggestion: Add logging: `except Exception as e: app.logger.warning(f'Auth secret load failed: {e}'); return ''`

findings.title: Inadequate regex validation for GCP Project IDs
findings.description: The PROJECT_RE regex does not enforce strict GCP project ID requirements. GCP project IDs cannot contain consecutive hyphens or end with hyphens. The validation is too permissive.
findings.fix_suggestion: Validate against GCP API requirements or use stricter regex that checks for consecutive/trailing hyphens

findings.title: Recursion in classification script without depth limit
findings.description: The `removesubgroups()` and `addsubgroups()` recursive functions have no depth limit or cycle detection. A circular parent-child relationship in the JSON data would cause infinite recursion and stack overflow.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add recursion depth limit and visited set for cycle detection

findings.title: No error handling for file I/O in classification transformation
findings.description: The Python classification transformation script opens files but does not handle FileNotFoundError, IOError, or JSONDecodeError. If a backup file is missing or corrupted, the script crashes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add try-except around file operations with clear error messages

findings.title: Global mutable variable for passphrase caching not thread-safe
findings.description: The `_cached_passphrase` global variable is modified without locks. In a Flask app with concurrent requests, this could lead to race conditions where multiple threads read/write the cached value simultaneously.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use functools.lru_cache or thread-safe cache mechanism

findings.title: No null/None safety checks in Terraform state file parsing
findings.description: The `read_metadata()` and `read_outputs()` functions assume JSON structure without validating that required keys exist. If GCS returns malformed JSON, the code could crash with KeyError.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use meta.get() with defaults instead of direct key access

findings.title: Insecure passphrase comparison timing vulnerability
findings.description: The passphrase verification uses simple string equality (`token == passphrase`), which is vulnerable to timing attacks. An attacker could measure response time to brute-force the passphrase.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use secrets.compare_digest() for timing-safe comparison

lint.output: /home/merm/projects/portis-app/src/app/contact/page.tsx 23:13 warning Using `` could result in slower LCP and higher bandwidth. Consider using `` from `next/image` or a custom image loader to automatically optimize images. This may incur additional usage or cost from your provider. See: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/no-img-element @next/next/no-img-element ✖ 1 problem (0 errors, 1 warning)

findings.title: Weak Random Number Generation for Verification Codes
findings.description: The Lambda API handler uses Math.random() to generate verification codes used for email verification, password reset, and other critical security operations. Math.random() is cryptographically weak and predictable, making verification codes vulnerable to brute force attacks.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace Math.random() with crypto.randomBytes() for generating verification codes. Example: `const verifyCode = crypto.randomBytes(3).toString('hex').toUpperCase();` (generates 6-char hex codes). The crypto module is already imported.

findings.title: Known Vulnerabilities in fast-xml-parser Dependency
findings.description: The project has 2 moderate severity vulnerabilities in the fast-xml-parser dependency (versions 4.0.0-beta.3 through 5.5.6). The vulnerability is: 'Entity Expansion Limits Bypassed When Set to Zero Due to JavaScript Falsy Evaluation in fast-xml-parser'. This is indirectly used via @aws-sdk/xml-builder.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run `npm audit fix` to update fast-xml-parser to a patched version. The override in package.json (line 56) already specifies `fast-xml-parser >= 5.5.6`, but the constraint may be too loose. Update to the latest version and verify AWS SDK dependencies are compatible.

findings.title: Encryption Keys Cached in localStorage
findings.description: Encryption keys (containing sensitive cryptographic material) are cached in localStorage for 'unlock-on-refresh' functionality. While the master key is stored in memory only, the encryption key material stored in localStorage could be vulnerable if the browser's storage is compromised.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider using sessionStorage instead of localStorage to limit the lifetime of cached keys to the current session. Alternatively, implement a timeout mechanism that clears cached keys after 30 minutes of inactivity. Document this as a known limitation and advise users to re-enter their password after closing and reopening the browser for maximum security.

findings.title: Weak ID Generation in Client-Side Storage
findings.description: The storage.ts file uses Math.random() to generate IDs for client-side data. While this is not as critical as security-sensitive operations, it could lead to ID collisions in edge cases.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use `crypto.getRandomValues()` for the random portion or use a UUID library. Example: `return `${Date.now()}-${crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(4)).reduce((a,b) => a + b.toString(16), '')}`

findings.title: Unoptimized Image in Contact Page
findings.description: The contact page uses a standard HTML tag instead of Next.js Image component for the founder photo. This results in potential LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) impact and missed optimization opportunities.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace with Next.js Image component: `import Image from 'next/image'; ... <Image src="/cody-jo-eflin.jpg" alt="Cody Jo Eflin" className="h-auto w-full object-cover" width={400} height={500} priority />`

findings.title: Incomplete TODOs in Lambda Code
findings.description: The Lambda API handler contains TODO comments for incomplete features that are not yet wired up.
findings.fix_suggestion: Complete the web-push and Polly implementations when ready. These are intentionally incomplete and documented.

lint.output: ESLint found 3 errors and 25 warnings. Errors: 3x @next/next/no-html-link-for-pages in src/app/layout.tsx (lines 98-100). Warnings: unused variables, missing alt text, unused imports, missing useEffect dependencies.

tests.output: All 325 tests passed across 20 test files in Vitest.

findings.title: Weak Random Number Generator for Password Reset Codes
findings.description: The password reset code generation uses Math.random() instead of cryptographically secure random values. The generateCode() function generates 6-digit codes using Math.floor(100000 + Math.random() * 900000), which produces only 900,000 possible values. An attacker could brute force a 6-digit code in minutes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace with cryptographically secure random using crypto.getRandomValues(). Generate an 8-character alphanumeric code or longer numeric code with sufficient entropy.

findings.title: Multiple Critical Next.js Security Vulnerabilities in Dependencies
findings.description: npm audit identified 3 moderate severity vulnerabilities affecting Next.js 16.1.6: (1) HTTP request smuggling in rewrites (GHSA-ggv3-7p47-pfv8), (2) Unbounded next/image disk cache growth exhausting storage (GHSA-3x4c-7xq6-9pq8), (3) Unbounded postponed resume buffering DoS (GHSA-h27x-g6w4-24gq), (4) null origin CSRF bypass in Server Actions (GHSA-mq59-m269-xvcx), (5) null origin dev HMR CSRF bypass (GHSA-jcc7-9wpm-mj36).
findings.fix_suggestion: Update Next.js to 16.2.1 or later: npm audit fix --force. Verify CSRF protection uses Origin/Referer headers, not null origin trust.

findings.title: XML Entity Expansion Vulnerability in fast-xml-parser Dependency
findings.description: Transitive dependency fast-xml-parser 4.0.0-5.5.6 (via @aws-sdk/xml-builder) has vulnerability GHSA-jp2q-39xq-3w4g where entity expansion limits are bypassed when set to zero due to falsy evaluation. This enables billion-laughs DoS attacks.
findings.fix_suggestion: Run npm audit fix to update @aws-sdk/xml-builder. Ensure entity expansion is explicitly limited (non-zero) when parsing XML.

findings.title: Internal Navigation Using <a> Instead of <Link> Component
findings.description: src/app/layout.tsx lines 98-100 use <a> elements for internal navigation to /cards/, /guides/, and /blog/ instead of Next.js <Link /> component. This is an ESLint error (@next/next/no-html-link-for-pages).
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace with Next.js Link: import Link from 'next/link'; then <Link href="/path">Text</Link> for each of the 3 links.

findings.title: User Images Using Instead of Optimized next/image
findings.description: src/app/journal/entry/page.tsx line 442 and src/app/journal/view/page.tsx use elements instead of Next.js for user-uploaded journal attachments. ESLint warning @next/next/no-img-element.
findings.fix_suggestion: For user-uploaded images, either: (1) Use next/image with fill or fixed sizes, ensuring proper alt text and sizes are set; OR (2) Keep but suppress ESLint warning if user uploads don't justify optimization overhead.

findings.title: Missing useEffect Dependencies: Stale Closures
findings.description: Two React Hook violations: src/app/history/page.tsx line 95 (useEffect missing masterKey dependency) and src/app/reading/page.tsx line 525 (useEffect missing refreshSavedReadings). Can cause stale state in closures.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add missing dependencies to useEffect arrays: [masterKey] for history/page.tsx, [refreshSavedReadings] for reading/page.tsx.

findings.title: Multiple Unused Variables and Imports
findings.description: Scattered unused variables: lambda/api/admin.test.mjs (_origin), src/__tests__/auth-context.test.tsx (ReactNode), src/app/insights/page.tsx (CardMoodCorrelation), src/app/learn/page.tsx (useEffect, AuthGuard), src/app/library/page.tsx (AuthGuard), src/app/recap/page.tsx (i), src/lib/api.ts (content, prompts, mood, tags).
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove unused variables. If intentionally left for future use or to match a pattern, prefix with _ to silence linter.

findings.title: Image Placeholder Missing Alt Text
findings.description: src/app/journal/entry/page.tsx line 445 renders a fallback <Image> icon when uploaded image fails to load, but parent div has no alt text or aria-label. Violates WCAG 2.1 AA.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-label to parent div: aria-label="Image failed to load" or wrap in a semantic <figure> with <figcaption>.

findings.title: DynamoDB Full Table Scan in Admin List Users
findings.description: lambda/api/admin.mjs handleListUsers performs a Scan operation without proper indexing or filtering, scanning the entire DynamoDB table on every admin list request. No use of GSI for efficient queries.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use GSI1 (email-based) with Query instead of Scan. Implement proper cursor-based pagination with ExclusiveStartKey.

findings.title: Console Errors Logged Without PII Filtering
findings.description: Multiple console.error() calls throughout src/ and lambda/ (history/page.tsx, daily/page.tsx, journal/page.tsx, etc.) log error details to console/CloudWatch without sanitization. Could leak sensitive data in stack traces.
findings.fix_suggestion: Log only err.message, not full err objects. Use structured logging and filter PII before logging. Consider using a logging service with automatic PII masking.

findings.title: Encryption Key Handling: No Runtime Guard Against Accidental Logging
findings.description: src/lib/journal-crypto.ts exports toBase64/fromBase64 functions used throughout encryption. No runtime assertions prevent accidental logging of decrypted content if these functions are called with plaintext data.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add optional JSDoc warnings and dev-time assertions that toBase64 is never called with plaintext. Or seal functions in closure to prevent external misuse.
